msgs.text:
Turn 55 — final proof package after D.3 closes I re-read the three current attachments only. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G and relators rS,rH, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS. The problem statement and prompt ask exactly for this over-G isomorphism decision.The state after Turn 54 is now materially different from the earlier “proof modulo certificates” stage. The last executable verifier gap, D.3, is recorded as closed: the actual A2=R/(4,t) coefficient-Bockstein row μ4B4 was computed, and appending it to D5,6,7 gives the rank jump rankD5,6,7=60,rank(μ4B4D5,6,7)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed μ4=1 scalar to μ4=0. Thus the final nonlinear certificate block is closed in the sweep ledger.So Turn 55 can now state the proof as a single theorem.

Theorem

There is a continuous over-G isomorphismΓS≅GΓH.Equivalently, there is a continuous automorphism Φ:F→F over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH,and therefore Φ descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.Proof package

Fix the source orientationρS:=rS−1.

This is harmless because rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup. The goal is therefore to build a twisted Nielsen automorphism of F=P⋊G over G carrying ρS to rH.The abelianized starting point is already invertible. OverΛ=Z2 [[G]],

Turn 1 computed the relation rowswS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E),using ρS=rS−1. The row comparison splits by the central idempotent E. On the (1−E)-piece the rows swap; on the Ef-piece, with s=σ2, the matrixU0=( s−1s−212)has determinant s, hence is a unit, and carries (s+3,−2) to (s2+1,s−1). Together with the E(1−f)-piece, these matrices glue to some

U∈GL2(Λ)carrying wS to wH.

After this H1 -change, all non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled. The only surviving obstruction tower is the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 Magnus problem. In that residual problem, Turn 18 established the uniform inductionCm=Lm (m≥5),so every odd primitive layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W↦W[2].The coefficient lift over the Ef-local ring is also closed. Locally,

R=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

The raw row(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is Nielsen-equivalent over R to the normalized row (2,t),so the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact. The first t-layer is contractible; the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4);and higher (2,t)-adic layers are killed by the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade, ordered by Magnus root degree first.The one remaining coefficient concern before Turn 54 was the A2=R/(4,t), d=4, μ4 -toggle. The fixed gauge gives μ4=1, but the Turn 54 verifier computes the actual Z/4 coefficient-2 ghosts and shows that μ4B4 is not in the row span of D5,6,7. Therefore some continuation-kernel element changes μ4 by 1, killing the fixed scalar. The nonlinear certificate ledger is now closed: D.1 has explicit degree-10 Bockstein witnesses, D.2 has the residual degree-16 lower-base toggle, D.3 is the 60→61 stack-rank certificate, and D.4 is the d=8 quotient witness θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].Now use finite filtered Nielsen compactness. For a degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, define coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J. The finite solution sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data with first layer UmodJ, satisfying the cocycle relation for τσ=τ2, and sending ρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J. Each finite datum gives an actual finite over-G automorphism: the first layer is invertible, so the finite Frattini criterion applies to the finite 2-group P/PN,J.The residual induction, coefficient Koszul–Witt induction, and closed nonlinear certificate ledger proveSN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation. These finite nonempty sets are functorial under refinement, so compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit system. The limit is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→FwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 have the same closed normal closure,Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Thus Φ descends toΓS≅ΓH.Because every finite-stage map is over G, the limit and descended isomorphism are over G, soπH∘Φ=πS.This is exactly the desired conclusion.

Structural audit The final proof no longer rests on hammering a single exceptional case. The d=4 coefficient issue is resolved by the same uniform lesson as the residual degree-16 issue: a fixed gauge can exhibit a false obstruction, but the full lower-plus-continuation torsor moves the class. Turn 54’s 60→61 stack-rank is the executable version of that principle.The only caveat is expository, not mathematical: the attached history records the verifier outputs, but the actual verifier scripts are not among the three current attachments. So within the sweep ledger, the certificates are closed; for a standalone paper or appendix, the scripts or matrices should be included explicitly.

ESTABLISHED: The sweep now proves ΓS≅GΓH. The last outstanding executable certificate, D.3, is recorded as verified by the rank jump 60→61, so the finite coefficient nonemptiness and filtered Nielsen compactness argument are fully supported within the attached history.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains. The only remaining gap is presentation/reproducibility outside this transcript: include the verifier code or explicit matrices in a standalone appendix.

NEXT: Write the final standalone note: definitions; H1 Nielsen matrix; residual restricted-Lie induction; coefficient Koszul–Witt induction; certificates D.1–D.4, including Turn 54’s stack-rank output; filtered Nielsen compactness; quotient descent.[TURN55-FINAL-PROOF-PACKAGE-ISO]
